metadata.mission: Build a Robot Framework black-box integration test suite running entirely in Docker, backed by a living scenario catalog (CATALOG.md) that provides full traceability from user stories to test descriptions to Robot Framework test cases.

metadata.description: Confirm exact stdout/stderr output format of the caldawarrior binary and identify which CLI behaviors exist today. Produces ground truth for all Robot Framework assertions and a gap analysis vs existing Rust tests.

task-1-1.title: Verify CLI output format and behavior coverage

metadata.description: Read src/output.rs fully and extract exact stdout format strings. Check which behaviors are implemented: bad-auth exit code, config permission warning, recurring task warning, cyclic dependency warning, CalDAV RELATED-TO to TW depends reverse mapping. Catalog all 12 existing Rust integration tests noting library-level vs CLI-level coverage. Document all findings in tests/robot/docs/GAP_ANALYSIS.md including a Verified Output Formats section with exact strings and a list of behaviors to tag skip-unimplemented in Phase 5.
acceptance_criteria: Config file schema documented: all required keys verified against src/config.rs (server_url, username, password, [[calendar]] with project and url field names confirmed)
caldavuid UDA key name and type verified against caldawarrior source
Zero-write summary line format documented for both directions (for S-12 and S-22 assertions)
Exact stdout success summary pattern documented and traceable to src/output.rs
Dry-run prefix and summary formats documented
Warning and error line formats documented
All unimplemented behaviors explicitly flagged for skip-unimplemented tagging
All 12 existing Rust tests catalogued with coverage type

metadata.description: Manually review GAP_ANALYSIS.md to ensure every output format string is traceable to src/output.rs and at least 10 uncovered behaviors are listed with scenario ID mapping.

Create tests/robot/docs/CATALOG.md — the authoritative living document with user stories, test scenario descriptions, and Robot Framework test case name references. Output format strings must come from Phase 1 findings.

Create tests/robot/docs/CATALOG.md with ~30 scenarios across 7 categories. Include a header explaining the traceability chain (user story → catalog entry → .robot test case name) and the scenario ID convention (gaps between ranges reserved). Each entry: ID, Category, User Story (2-4 sentence narrative in plain language), Setup, Expected Outcome (stdout pattern from Phase 1, stderr pattern, exit code, CalDAV state, TW state), Robot Test Case Name. Categories and ID ranges: First Sync (S-01 to S-05), LWW Conflict (S-10 to S-14), Orphan and Deletion (S-20 to S-22), Status Mapping (S-30 to S-33), Dependencies (S-40 to S-42), CLI Behavior (S-50 to S-55), Field Mapping (S-60 to S-63). Output patterns must reference Phase 1 GAP_ANALYSIS.md. Scenarios for unimplemented behaviors are included but marked skip-unimplemented.
acceptance_criteria: 28 or more scenarios with all fields filled
Output patterns sourced from Phase 1 verified format not guessed
Each scenario has a Robot test case name matching exactly what will be in the .robot file
User stories readable by a non-developer
skip-unimplemented scenarios explicitly marked in catalog

metadata.description: Read CATALOG.md top to bottom and verify every scenario is understandable without reading the code.
Create the self-contained Docker environment: a multi-stage Dockerfile (Rust builder + RF runner) and a docker-compose.yml with Radicale healthcheck. No host Rust, Python, or TaskWarrior required.

tests/robot/Dockerfile. Stage 1 (builder): FROM rust:1.76-bookworm (pinned, not latest), WORKDIR /build, copy Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, src/. Use BuildKit cache mounts: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/local/cargo/registry --mount=type=cache,target=/build/target cargo build --release. Stage 2 (runner): FROM debian:bookworm-slim, install python3 python3-pip curl gnupg taskwarrior, pip install with exact pinned versions: robotframework==7.0.1 icalendar==5.0.12 requests==2.31.0 (record pip freeze in a requirements.txt comment for auditability). The icalendar package is required for CalDAVLibrary GET-parse-mutate-PUT operations. Create non-root user: RUN useradd -m testrunner and USER testrunner. Copy caldawarrior binary from builder. WORKDIR /tests. No Rust toolchain in runner stage. Pin Rust image version with a comment noting the date selected and a Makefile target update-docker-pins as the deliberate upgrade path.
acceptance_criteria: docker build -f tests/robot/Dockerfile . succeeds from repo root with DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1
Built image has caldawarrior, task, and robot on PATH
whoami inside container returns testrunner not root
Image does not contain Rust toolchain
Both base images pinned to specific versions not latest tags

metadata.description: tests/robot/docker-compose.yml. Service radicale: image tomsquest/docker-radicale:3.3.0.0 (pinned, not latest), mounts ./radicale.config and ./htpasswd read-only, TZ=UTC, healthcheck with curl -f -u testuser:testpassword http://localhost:5232/ interval 2s retries 15. Service robot: build context ../.. (repo root) with dockerfile tests/robot/Dockerfile, depends_on radicale condition service_healthy, environment RADICALE_URL=http://radicale:5232 RADICALE_USER=testuser RADICALE_PASSWORD=testpassword TZ=UTC, mounts ./:/tests:ro and ./results:/results, command robot --outputdir /results /tests/suites/. Radicale port is NOT exposed to the host (robot reaches it via Docker internal network only, avoiding port 5232 conflicts on the host).
acceptance_criteria: docker compose -f tests/robot/docker-compose.yml run --rm robot robot --version exits 0
Robot container waits for Radicale healthcheck before starting
Results written to tests/robot/results/ with correct non-root ownership
TZ=UTC set on both containers
Radicale image pinned to specific version not latest
Radicale port not exposed to host

metadata.description: Create tests/robot/radicale.config (basic auth, owner_only rights, filesystem storage, htpasswd_encryption = plain). Create tests/robot/htpasswd with literal content testuser:testpassword — add comment at top: TEST CREDENTIALS ONLY DO NOT REUSE IN ANY REAL ENVIRONMENT. Add tests/robot/results/ to .gitignore. Create tests/robot/results/.gitkeep. Create .dockerignore at repo root excluding .git/, target/, tests/robot/results/, and any *.md files not needed by the build — only Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, and src/ are needed by the Dockerfile COPY statements; excluding target/ is critical as it can be several gigabytes and cause non-reproducible builds.
acceptance_criteria: Radicale healthcheck passes with testuser:testpassword
htpasswd file has TEST CREDENTIALS ONLY comment
tests/robot/results/ gitignored with only .gitkeep tracked
.dockerignore at repo root excludes .git/ target/ and tests/robot/results/
docker build context size is small (target/ excluded)

metadata.description: Run docker compose -f tests/robot/docker-compose.yml run --rm robot robot --version and confirm it exits 0 and prints RF version.

Implement CalDAVLibrary.py, TaskWarriorLibrary.py, and common.robot. These three files are the foundation all test suites depend on. Can be authored in parallel with Phase 3 but execution testing requires Phase 3 complete.

tests/robot/resources/CalDAVLibrary.py. Class-based Robot keyword library. Constructor reads RADICALE_URL, RADICALE_USER, RADICALE_PASSWORD from environment. Uses requests for HTTP and icalendar for iCal parsing. Keywords: Create Collection (MKCOL, returns collection URL), Delete Collection (DELETE), Count VTODOs (PROPFIND depth:1, counts .ics hrefs), Put VTODO (PUT uid.ics), Get VTODO Raw (GET returns ical text), Delete VTODO, Modify VTODO Summary (GET-parse with icalendar-mutate-serialize-PUT), Modify VTODO Status (sets STATUS and COMPLETED timestamp), Get VTODO Property (icalendar parser), VTODO Should Exist, VTODO Should Have Property. All HTTP errors raise Robot AssertionError with descriptive messages. All keywords have Robot-compatible docstrings.
acceptance_criteria: Create Collection + Put VTODO + Count VTODOs round-trip works against live Radicale
Modify VTODO Summary correctly round-trips via icalendar library not regex
All keywords have Robot-compatible docstrings

metadata.description: tests/robot/resources/TaskWarriorLibrary.py. Class-based Robot keyword library. All subprocess calls set both TASKDATA=<path> and TASKRC=<path>/.taskrc to fully override system config. Keywords: Set TW Data Dir (creates dir, sets instance _tw_env, writes .taskrc with data.location, uda.caldavuid.type=string, uda.caldavuid.label=CaldavUID, confirmation=no, json.array=on), Add TW Task (parses Created task N, exports to get UUID, returns uuid), Get TW Task (task export rc.json.array=on <uuid>, returns dict), Complete TW Task, Delete TW Task, TW Task Count, TW Task Should Have Caldavuid, TW Task Should Have Status, TW Task Should Have Field, Clear TW Data (removes task data files preserves directory).
acceptance_criteria: Add TW Task + Get TW Task round-trip returns correct description
Set TW Data Dir writes valid .taskrc with caldavuid UDA configured
Two instances with different data dirs do not share task state
Both TASKDATA and TASKRC env vars set in all subprocess calls

metadata.description: tests/robot/resources/common.robot. Suite Setup: generates UUID slug, calls Create Collection sets COLLECTION_URL, calls Set TW Data Dir with /tmp/tw-UUID. Suite Teardown: Delete Collection, Clear TW Data, delete temp config dir. Test Teardown: Clear TW Data (per-test TW reset). Run Caldawarrior Sync keyword: writes temp config to /tmp/cw-config-SLUG.toml using config schema verified in Phase 1 (server_url, username, password, [[calendar]] project=default url=COLLECTION_URL). Runs caldawarrior --config PATH sync with TASKDATA and TASKRC env vars. Uses Run Process with return_rc=True to capture non-zero exit codes without raising, capturing stdout stderr and returncode. Deletes temp config immediately after capture. Sets LAST_STDOUT, LAST_STDERR, LAST_EXIT_CODE (initialized to empty and -1 in Suite Setup). Run Caldawarrior Sync Dry Run: same with --dry-run. Helper keywords: Stdout Should Contain, Stderr Should Contain, Exit Code Should Be. ZERO_WRITES_PATTERN constant defined from Phase 1 findings. skip-unimplemented tag semantics: tests use [Tags] skip-unimplemented combined with Skip keyword so they appear as SKIP not FAIL in the report; CI counts SKIP separately and does not fail the build on SKIP; each skipped test has a [Documentation] comment referencing the missing feature by name.
acceptance_criteria: Run Caldawarrior Sync on empty TW + CalDAV exits 0 and LAST_EXIT_CODE=0
LAST_STDOUT, LAST_STDERR, LAST_EXIT_CODE initialized in Suite Setup
No temp config files remain after Test Teardown
Non-zero exit codes captured without raising (return_rc=True)
ZERO_WRITES_PATTERN constant defined for S-12 and S-22
skip-unimplemented tagged tests appear as SKIP in HTML report not FAIL
Two sequential suites do not share TW state or CalDAV collection

verify-4-1.title: Verify keyword libraries with dry-run syntax check

metadata.description: Run robot --dryrun /tests/suites/ inside the Docker container to check for syntax errors in libraries and resources without executing real test steps.

Implement all 7 .robot suite files covering ~30 scenarios from CATALOG.md. Each suite: Settings block with imports and Suite Setup/Teardown, test cases named exactly as in CATALOG.md, Documentation tags with user story text. All suites depend on Phase 4 keyword libraries.

S-01 to S-05. S-01 First Sync Tasks Pushed to CalDAV: add 2 TW tasks, sync, assert CalDAV has 2 VTODOs, stdout matches success pattern, exit 0. S-02 caldavuid UDA Set After First Sync: sync 1 task, assert TW task has caldavuid. S-03 Dry-Run Does Not Write to CalDAV: dry-run with 1 TW task, assert CalDAV still 0, stdout contains [DRY-RUN]. S-04 Project Routing to Default Calendar: task with no project routes to default calendar (1 VTODO created). S-05 CalDAV-Only VTODO Pulled to TW: put VTODO directly in CalDAV, sync, assert TW has task with matching description.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 test cases pass
Each has [Documentation] tag matching CATALOG.md user story
S-03 asserts CalDAV count = 0 after dry-run
Test case names match CATALOG.md exactly

metadata.description: S-10 to S-14. S-10 TW Wins LWW Conflict: sync task, modify TW task, sync again, assert CalDAV VTODO updated with TW description. S-11 CalDAV Wins LWW Conflict: sync twice for stable point, modify CalDAV VTODO externally, sync again, assert TW task updated. S-12 Stable Sync Produces Zero Writes: sync twice, assert second sync stdout contains 0 created 0 updated in both directions. S-13 ETag Conflict Recovered Gracefully: modify CalDAV VTODO and TW task concurrently, sync, assert exit code 0. S-14 TW Task With No Modified Timestamp Syncs Correctly: create minimal TW task with entry only, sync, assert VTODO created in CalDAV, exit 0.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 test cases pass
S-12 explicitly asserts 0 created 0 updated in stdout

metadata.description: S-20 to S-22. S-20 CalDAV Deletion Removes TW Task: sync TW task, delete VTODO externally, sync, assert TW task deleted and CalDAV still 0. S-21 TW Deletion Removes CalDAV VTODO: sync TW task, delete TW task, sync, assert CalDAV 0 VTODOs. S-22 Large Dataset Stability: add 20 TW tasks, sync all to CalDAV, sync again, assert second sync stdout shows 0 writes.
acceptance_criteria: All 3 test cases pass
S-20 confirms CalDAV count = 0 after sync (VTODO not re-created)

metadata.description: S-30 to S-33. S-30 CalDAV COMPLETED Status Propagates to TW: push TW task, mark VTODO STATUS:COMPLETED in CalDAV, sync, assert TW task status = completed. S-31 TW Completed Task Propagates to CalDAV: sync TW task, complete TW task, sync, assert VTODO STATUS:COMPLETED. S-32 CalDAV CANCELLED Status Maps to TW Deleted: put VTODO with STATUS:CANCELLED, sync, assert TW task created with status deleted. S-33 Recurring VTODO Emits Warning and Is Skipped: put VTODO with RRULE, sync, assert stderr contains warning about recurring. Tag with skip-unimplemented if behavior not confirmed in Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: S-33 either passes or is tagged skip-unimplemented with explanatory comment

metadata.description: S-40 to S-42. S-40 TW Dependency Synced to CalDAV RELATED-TO: create tasks A and B where B depends on A, sync, assert B VTODO has RELATED-TO;RELTYPE=DEPENDS-ON pointing to A UID. S-41 CalDAV RELATED-TO Synced to TW Depends: put two VTODOs with RELATED-TO, sync, assert TW task has depends field. Tag skip-unimplemented if Phase 1 confirms reverse mapping not implemented. S-42 Cyclic Dependency Emits Warning: create cyclic TW dependency A to B to A, sync, assert stderr contains warning about cyclic, exit code 0.
acceptance_criteria: S-40 and S-42 pass
S-40 reads raw VTODO iCal text and checks RELATED-TO property string
S-41 passes or tagged skip-unimplemented per Phase 1 findings

metadata.description: S-50 to S-55. S-50 Success Output Format: sync 1 TW task, assert stdout matches exact success summary pattern from CATALOG.md. S-51 Dry-Run Output Format: dry-run with 1 task, assert stdout has [DRY-RUN] prefix lines and summary. S-52 Invalid Credentials Exit Code: wrong password in config, assert exit code 1 and stderr has error. S-53 Missing Config File Exit Code: --config /nonexistent, assert exit code 1 and stderr mentions config. S-54 Unmapped Project Warning: task project not in config calendars, assert stderr contains [WARN] and UnmappedProject. S-55 Config File Permission Warning: chmod config 0644, sync, assert stderr contains [WARN] and permission. Container runs as non-root testrunner user so chmod is effective.
acceptance_criteria: S-50 through S-55 pass
Error tests assert exit code = 1
S-55 reliable because container runs as non-root user

metadata.description: S-60 to S-63. S-60 Due Date Roundtrip: TW task with due date, sync, assert VTODO has DUE property, sync back, assert TW has same due within 1 second. S-61 Tags Roundtrip: TW tags home and urgent, sync, assert VTODO CATEGORIES contains both, sync back, assert TW has both tags. S-62 Priority Roundtrip: TW priority H, sync, VTODO PRIORITY:1, sync back, TW priority H. S-63 Wait Date Roundtrip: future wait date, sync, VTODO has X-TASKWARRIOR-WAIT, sync back, TW has wait field.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 test cases pass
Datetime comparisons allow plus or minus 1 second tolerance
Each test performs full bidirectional roundtrip TW to CalDAV to TW

metadata.description: Run docker compose -f tests/robot/docker-compose.yml run --rm robot and review HTML report. Verify all test case names match CATALOG.md exactly.
Full suite passes, HTML report is accessible, Makefile provides convenient targets, and README explains both test layers. CATALOG.md updated with implementation status.

task-6-1.title: Run full suite and fix failures

metadata.description: Run docker compose -f tests/robot/docker-compose.yml run --rm robot and review the HTML report in tests/robot/results/. Fix any test failures. If a test relies on a behavior not yet implemented in the binary, tag it skip-unimplemented with a comment referencing the missing feature. Do not delete any test.
acceptance_criteria: All tests pass or tagged skip-unimplemented with explanatory comment
HTML report opens and shows per-scenario results

metadata.description: For each scenario in CATALOG.md add a status marker: checkmark for passing, warning for skipped or unimplemented, X for failing with issue link. Update any test case names that changed during implementation to match actual .robot file names.
acceptance_criteria: Every scenario has a status marker
All test case names in catalog match actual .robot file case names

metadata.description: Create or update Makefile at repo root with targets: test-robot (creates tests/robot/results/ if absent then runs docker compose -f tests/robot/docker-compose.yml run --rm robot), test-integration (runs cargo test --test integration), test-all (runs both), build-robot (runs docker compose -f tests/robot/docker-compose.yml build). Include ## comments on each target for make help compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: make test-robot runs the full Robot Framework suite end-to-end
make build-robot builds the Docker images
Targets documented with ## comments

metadata.description: Add or update the Testing section in README.md explaining both test layers: (1) White-box Rust integration tests via make test-integration requiring Docker Compose v2. (2) Black-box Robot Framework behavioral tests via make test-robot, fully self-contained with no host Rust Python or TaskWarrior required. Note Docker Compose v2 plugin requirement (docker compose not docker-compose). Link to tests/robot/docs/CATALOG.md for scenario documentation.
acceptance_criteria: README explains both test layers clearly
make test-robot is the documented invocation
Docker Compose v2 requirement stated
Link to CATALOG.md present

metadata.description: Compare full implementation against this spec. Verify all 7 suite files exist, CATALOG.md has status markers, Makefile targets work, and README is updated.

journal.title: Status changed to in_progress
journal.content: Starting investigation: reading src/output.rs, src/config.rs, and existing Rust tests to produce GAP_ANALYSIS.md
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Task Completed: Verify CLI output format and behavior coverage
journal.content: Created tests/robot/docs/GAP_ANALYSIS.md with full investigation results. All acceptance criteria met: (1) Config schema verified — server_url/username/password top-level, [[calendar]] with project/url fields confirmed against src/config.rs. (2) caldavuid UDA: key name 'caldavuid', type 'string', label 'CaldavUID', source src/tw_adapter.rs:187-193. (3) Zero-write summary: same format string as normal summary with all counters at 0 ('Synced: 0 created, 0 updated in CalDAV; 0 created, 0 updated in TW'). (4) Success summary traced to src/output.rs:31-35. (5) Dry-run prefix/summary formats documented from src/output.rs:82-118 and :43-47. (6) Warning/error formats documented including config permission warning (eprintln! in src/config.rs:95 — differs from generic [WARN] pattern). (7) 6 unimplemented behaviors flagged for skip-unimplemented. (8) 18 Rust tests catalogued (12 scenario tests in 3 files + 6 harness utility tests in mod.rs; task description said 12 but actual is 18 — deviation documented). Both forward and reverse RELATED-TO/depends mappings are implemented. Basic verification: file created, markdown valid."
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Task Completed: Review gap analysis completeness
journal.content: Manual review passed (accepted as-is). All output format strings cite src/output.rs line numbers. 23 behaviors documented total: 6 unimplemented (skip-unimplemented candidates) + 17 implemented but CLI-uncovered behaviors. Scenario ID mappings deferred to Phase 2 where scenario IDs are defined — accepted per user decision since scenarios do not exist yet at Phase 1.
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Status changed to in_progress
journal.content: Writing CATALOG.md with 28+ scenarios using verified format strings from GAP_ANALYSIS.md
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Task Completed: Create scenario catalog
journal.content: Created tests/robot/docs/CATALOG.md (1,436 lines, 30 scenarios). All acceptance criteria met: 30 scenarios (≥28), all output patterns sourced from GAP_ANALYSIS.md with §references, each scenario has a Robot Test Case Name, user stories written in plain language, 5 skip-unimplemented scenarios explicitly marked (S-42, S-50, S-51, S-52, S-53). Scenario ID ranges: First Sync S-01–S-05, LWW S-10–S-14, Orphan/Deletion S-20–S-22, Status S-30–S-33, Dependencies S-40–S-42, CLI Behavior S-50–S-55, Field Mapping S-60–S-63. Summary table at end for quick reference."
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Status changed to in_progress
journal.content: Amending CATALOG.md: adding bulk operation tests (S-70–S-79) and multi-sync journey scenarios (S-80–S-89)
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Task Completed: Review catalog readability
journal.content: Manual review passed after amendment. CATALOG.md expanded from 30 to 42 scenarios per user feedback: added Bulk Operations (S-70–S-75: multi-task first sync, mixed sync, stable point, dry run, orphan bulk cleanup) and Multi-Sync Journeys (S-80–S-85: incremental growth from TW side, CalDAV side, bidirectional, repeated edit cycles from each side, full lifecycle). All scenarios readable by non-developers. 37 active + 5 skip-unimplemented."
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Completed: Create multi-stage Dockerfile
journal.content: Created tests/robot/Dockerfile: multi-stage build — Stage 1 rust:1.76-bookworm with BuildKit cache mounts; Stage 2 debian:bookworm-slim with pinned pip deps (robotframework==7.0.1, icalendar==5.0.12, requests==2.31.0), non-root testrunner user, WORKDIR /tests. Binary copied from builder. All acceptance criteria met.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Completed: Create docker-compose.yml
journal.content: Created tests/robot/docker-compose.yml: radicale (tomsquest/docker-radicale:3.3.0.0, no host port, healthcheck, TZ=UTC); robot (depends_on service_healthy, RADICALE_URL/USER/PASSWORD, ./:/tests:ro + ./results:/results, robot --outputdir /results /tests/suites/). All acceptance criteria met.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Completed: Create Radicale config and credentials
journal.content: Created radicale.config (htpasswd plain, owner_only, filesystem, 0.0.0.0:5232), htpasswd (TEST CREDENTIALS warning + testuser:testpassword), results/.gitkeep. Updated .gitignore with negation pattern. Created .dockerignore excluding .git/, target/, tests/robot/results/, *.md. All acceptance criteria met.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Status changed to in_progress
journal.content: Running docker compose build and robot --version to verify the environment boots
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Task Completed: Verify Docker environment boots
journal.content: Docker environment verified. All acceptance criteria met: robot --version → RF 7.0.1, whoami → testrunner, caldawarrior on PATH (0.1.0), task on PATH (2.6.2), Radicale healthcheck passed. Three fixes applied to Dockerfile vs. initial spec: (1) rust:1.76→rust:1.85 (edition=2024 requires Rust ≥1.85); (2) pip install --break-system-packages (Debian Bookworm PEP 668 blocks system pip); (3) binary copied out of BuildKit cache mount via cp /build/target/release/caldawarrior /build/caldawarrior before stage ends (cache mount contents not visible to COPY --from=builder)."
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Completed: Implement CalDAVLibrary.py
journal.content: Created tests/robot/resources/CalDAVLibrary.py (360 lines). All 11 keywords implemented: Create Collection, Delete Collection, Count VTODOs, Put VTODO, Get VTODO Raw, Delete VTODO, Modify VTODO Summary (via icalendar not regex), Modify VTODO Status, Get VTODO Property, VTODO Should Exist, VTODO Should Have Property. ROBOT_LIBRARY_SCOPE=SUITE, requests.Session with Basic Auth, all errors raised as AssertionError. Syntax validated.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Completed: Implement TaskWarriorLibrary.py
journal.content: Created tests/robot/resources/TaskWarriorLibrary.py (349 lines). All 11 keywords implemented. All subprocess calls use TASKDATA+TASKRC from self._tw_env. set_tw_data_dir writes .taskrc with caldavuid UDA. add_tw_task parses 'Created task N.' then exports by number for UUID. clear_tw_data removes *.data files preserving dir and .taskrc. ROBOT_LIBRARY_SCOPE=SUITE. Syntax validated.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Completed: Implement common.robot
journal.content: Created tests/robot/resources/common.robot (91 lines). All required keywords: Suite Setup (UUID slug, isolated CalDAV collection + TW data dir), Suite Teardown, Test Teardown (TW.Clear TW Data), Run Caldawarrior Sync + Dry Run variant, Stdout/Stderr/ExitCode assertion helpers, Sync Should Produce Zero Writes, Skip If Unimplemented. ZERO_WRITES_PATTERN set from GAP_ANALYSIS §1.2. skip-unimplemented semantics documented in comment block.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Task Completed: Verify keyword libraries with dry-run syntax check
journal.content: robot --dryrun passes: 1 test, 1 passed, 0 failed, no import errors. Two fixes applied: (1) robot files starting with _ are ignored by RF — renamed stub to syntax_check.robot; (2) CalDAVLibrary/TaskWarriorLibrary not on PYTHONPATH — added PYTHONPATH: /tests/resources to docker-compose.yml robot service environment. Stub suite tests/robot/suites/syntax_check.robot created as minimal import verifier (will be retained for Phase 5)."
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Completed: Implement 01_first_sync.robot
journal.content: Created tests/robot/suites/01_first_sync.robot (65 lines, 5 test cases): First Sync Creates TW Task In CalDAV, First Sync Sets Caldavuid UDA On TW Task, First Sync Dry Run Does Not Write VTODOs, First Sync Routes Projectless Task To Default Calendar, Five CalDAV VTODOs Created In TW On First Sync. All names match CATALOG.md exactly.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Completed: Implement 02_lww_conflict.robot
journal.content: Created tests/robot/suites/02_lww_conflict.robot (83 lines, 5 test cases): TW Wins LWW Conflict Resolution, CalDAV Wins LWW Conflict Resolution, Immediate Re-Sync After Conflict Is Stable Point, ETag Conflict Is Handled Without Error, LWW Conflict Dry Run Shows Update Without Writing. Dict access via ${task}[caldavuid] RF7 syntax.
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Completed: Implement 03_orphan.robot
journal.content: Created tests/robot/suites/03_orphan.robot (62 lines, 3 test cases): Orphaned Caldavuid Causes TW Task Deletion, Externally Deleted CalDAV VTODO Causes TW Deletion, Re-Sync After Deletion Is Stable Point. Sync Should Produce Zero Writes used per GAP_ANALYSIS §1.1 (deletes not in summary counters). TW.TW Task Count used to verify deletion (avoids Get TW Task on deleted tasks).
journal.author: claude-code

journal.title: Status changed to in_progress
journal.content: Implementing 04_status_mapping.robot — S-30 through S-33 following CATALOG.md as source of truth. Patterns sourced from 01/02/03 sibling suites. CalDAVLibrary.modify_vtodo_status available for S-30.
journal.author: foundry-mcp

journal.title: Task Completed: Implement 04_status_mapping.robot
